user_message: Patricia Kim at Liberty's Delightful Sinful Bakery just sent this. Help me create the summary.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Patricia Kim <patricia.kim@libertysdeli.com>
To: Marcus Williams <marcus.williams@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-13 16:47 EST
Subject: URGENT: account health Issue at Liberty's Delightful Sinful Bakery

Just checking in on the account health proposal. Our Director of IT is asking for a status update before the board meeting next week.

---
From: Marcus Williams <marcus.williams@microsoft.com>
To: Patricia Kim <patricia.kim@libertysdeli.com>
Date: 2024-02-07 10:44 EST
Subject: Re: account health Discussion - Next Steps

There's been a change in our timeline for account health. Can we discuss the implications on the overall project plan?

---
From: Patricia Kim <patricia.kim@libertysdeli.com>
To: Marcus Williams <marcus.williams@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-07 12:00 EST
Subject: Re: account health Discussion - Next Steps

Our team encountered some challenges with account health. Could you provide additional documentation or schedule a technical review?

signals.detail: Signal detected for Liberty's Delightful Sinful Bakery: the cost projections need to factor in reserved instance pricing for accuracy

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF42-A trap (Treats casual workload mention as commitment); produces complete Signal assessment, opportunity hypothesis with confidence, ownership routing, response draft, next-s
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF42-A failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF42-A failure mode (Treats casual workload mention as commitment)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct account health decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF42-A -- Treats casual workload mention as commitment
